title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## Household Economic Survey population rebase: Year ended June 2019 to 2024
Read about revisions to weights in the Household Economic Survey (HES) for the years ended June 2019 to 2024, and the effects on estimates including HES population, household income and expenditure, and child poverty measures.

## Guide to mortality measures
Describes a range of mortality measures, discusses the strengths and limitations of these measures, and identifies where published measures are available.

## International travel: November 2025
International travel covers the number and characteristics of overseas visitors and New Zealand resident travellers (short-term movements) entering or leaving New Zealand.

## International migration: November 2025
International migration statistics give the latest outcomes-based measure of migration, which includes estimates of migrants entering or leaving New Zealand.

## Dwelling and household estimates: December 2025 quarter
National dwelling and household estimates are used for many purposes including planning, policy formation, business decisions, and as ‘bottom lines’ in the calculation of market coverage rates.

## Household labour force survey estimated working-age population: December 2025 quarter
The household labour force survey estimated working-age population table shows the population benchmarks used to produce household labour force survey estimates for the upcoming labour market statistics release.

## Guide to fertility measures
Describes a range of fertility measures, discusses the strengths and limitations of these measures, and identifies where published measures are available.

## Technical feasibility: Measuring population and dwellings using administrative data (2025 update)
Provides an update on the technical feasibility of using administrative (admin) data, supported by surveys, to measure New Zealand’s population and dwellings in the next census.

## Quality of geographic information in the experimental administrative population census (APC)
Provides an overview of how population counts at territorial authorities (and Auckland local board areas) and statistical area 2 (SA2) compare across the APC, 2023 Census data, and the official estimated resident population (ERP). Population demographics have been restricted to age, sex, and level 1 ethnicity.

## International travel: October 2025
International travel covers the number and characteristics of overseas visitors and New Zealand resident travellers (short-term movements) entering or leaving New Zealand.

## International migration: October 2025
International migration statistics give the latest outcomes-based measure of migration, which includes estimates of migrants entering or leaving New Zealand.

## LEED annual technical notes – 2025 update
Technical notes about the annual statistics produced from the Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED).
